assertions.label: allowable flexural tension Ft (Type S hollow)

raw.title: Unreinforced CMU Bearing Wall — ASD
raw.body_text: Project 💾 Save📂 Load 🖨 Summary📄 Full Calc⊞ Expand All⊝ Collapse Unreinforced CMU Bearing Wall — ASD Unreinforced CMU Bearing Wall — ASD 4″ & 6″ Nominal CMU · Combined Axial + Wind Flexure · Allowable Stress Design per TMS 402-22 TMS 402-22 Chapter 8 §8.2.4 Allowable Axial §8.2.5 Flexural Tension §8.2.6 Combined Loading MDG-2022 WALL SCHEMATIC w = 25.0 psf P = 1.0 k/ft δ h = 9.0' t = 5.625" Top support Btm. support Elevation hollow core t = 5.625" 12" (1 ft) tfs = 1.00" 6" nom. CMU (hollow) Cross Section (per ft) ■ Wall Type & Material TMS 402-22 §8.2 Nominal Wall Thickness 4″ Nominal CMU (actual 3.625″) 6″ Nominal CMU (actual 5.625″) Actual t = 5.625″ · An = 24 in²/ft · S = 46.3 in³/ft · r = 1.624″ Grout Condition Hollow (Ungrouted) Fully Grouted Face-shell bedding for hollow; gross section for grouted Specified Masonry Strength f′m (psi) Typical: 1500–2000 psi for CMU Mortar Type Type S Type N Affects Ft per TMS 402-22 Table 8.2.5.2 ■ Geometry & Loading ASD Load Combination Wall Height h (ft) Clear height between supports Max ASD Vertical Load P (plf) Total axial load at critical section (lb per ft of wall). For tension check, re-run with min P. Interior Wind Pressure w (psf) Lateral pressure on wall face Support Conditions Simply Supported — M = wh²/8 Fixed-Pinned — M = wh²/10 Fixed-Fixed — M = wh²/12 Most conservative and common: simply supported Apply ⅓ stress increase for wind load combination (TMS 402-22 §8.1.4.1) — multiply allowable stresses by 4/3 ▶ Run All Checks ✓ ALL CHECKS PASS Governing D/C = 0.96 · 6″ CMU — Hollow (Ungrouted) · h = 9.0 ft · fʼm = 1500.0 psi · P = 1000.0 plf · w = 25.0 psf SECTION PROPERTIES — PER FOOT OF WALL Property Symbol Value Notes Nominal thickness tnom 6″ Actual thickness t 5.6 in CMU modular dimension Net area An 24.0 in²/ft Face-shell bedding: 2 × t₟s × 12 Net section modulus Sn 46.3 in³/ft Iₙ/(t/2) Net moment of inertia In 130.4 in⁴/ft t³ − (t−2t₟s)³ Radius of gyration r 1.6 in t/√12 (simplified per TMS) Modulus of elasticity Em 1350000.0 psi 900 × fʼm (TMS 402-22 §4.2.2) Face shell thickness tfs 1.0 in Min. per TMS 602-22 Wall weight wwall 38.0 psf Approximate h/r slenderness h/r 66.5 Short: use Eq. (8-16) h/t slenderness h/t 19.2 OK ≤ 20 KEY DEMANDS & ALLOWABLES 41.7 psi Actual Axial Stress fₐ = P/Aₙ 290.4 psi Allow. Axial Stress Fₐ (eff.) 253.1 ft‑lb/ft Wind Moment at Mid-Height Mₘₐˣ 65.6 psi Actual Flexural Stress fᵇ 500.0 psi Allow. Flexural Stress Fᵇ (eff.) 0.27 Combined Unity fₐ/Fₐ + fᵇ/Fᵇ 23.9 psi Net Flexural Tension fᵇ − fₐ 25.0 psi Allow. Flex. Tension Fₜ (eff.) CODE CHECKS Check Reference Demand Capacity D/C Status Detail Slenderness — h/t Max h/t = 20 for unreinforced bearing walls TMS 402-22 §5.5.1.1 h/t = 19.2 Limit = 20 0.96 PASS ▶ Calc TMS 402-22 §5.5.1.1 — Slenderness (h/t) Limit: h/t ≤ 20 for unreinforced bearing walls h = 9.0 ft = 108.0 in t = 5.6 in (actual) h/t = 108.0 / 5.6 = 19.2 D/C = 19.2 20.0 = 0.96 Axial Stress fa vs Fa h/r = 66.5 TMS 402-22 §8.2.4.3 fa = 41.7 psi Fa = 290.4 psi 0.14 PASS ▶ Calc TMS 402-22 §8.2.4.3 — Allowable Axial Compressive Stress Eq. (8-16): F a = 0.25 fʼm × [1 − h 140r ²] when h/r ≤ 99 h/r = 108.0/1.6 = 66.5 ≤ 99 ✓ = 0.25 × 1500.0 × [1 − (108.0/(140 × 1.6)²] = 290.4 psi (base) × k = 1.00 → 290.4 psi (eff.) fa = P/An = 1000.0 / 24.0 = 41.7 psi D/C = 41.7 290.4 = 0.14 Flexural Stress fb vs Fb M = 253.1 ft-lb/ft (w = 25.0 psf, h = 9.0 ft) TMS 402-22 §8.2.3 fb = 65.6 psi Fb = 500.0 psi 0.13 PASS ▶ Calc TMS 402-22 §8.2.3, Eq. (8-13) — Allowable Flexural Compressive Stress Support condition: Simply Supported — M max = w·h²/8 Mmax = w × h²/8 = 25.0 × 9.0²/8 = 253.1 ft‑lb/ft = 253.1 × 12 = 3037.5 in‑lb/ft fb = M/S n = 3037.5 / 46.3 = 65.6 psi Eq. (8-13): F b = 1 3 × fʼm = 1 3 × 1500.0 = 500.0 psi (base) × 1.00 = 500.0 psi (eff.) D/C = 65.6 500.0 = 0.13 Combined Compression — Unity Check fₐ/Fₐ = 0.143 + fᵇ/Fᵇ = 0.131 TMS 402-22 §8.2.6.2 fa Fa + fb Fb ≤ 1.0 0.27 PASS ▶ Calc TMS 402-22 §8.2.6.2 — Combined Compressive Stresses Eq. (8-20): fa Fa + fb Fb ≤ 1.0 = 41.7 290.4 + 65.6 500.0 = 0.143 + 0.131 = 0.275 ≤ 1.0 ✓ D/C (Unity) = 0.275 Net Flexural Tension (fb − fa) vs Ft Fₜ = 25 psi base × 1.00 TMS 402-22 §8.2.5 fnet = 23.9 psi Ft = 25.0 psi 0.96 PASS ▶ Calc TMS 402-22 §8.2.5 / §8.2.6.3 — Net Flexural Tension Net tensile stress: f net = f b − f a = 65.6 − 41.7 = 23.9 psi Ft = 25 psi (base, TMS 402-22 Table 8.2.5.2) × 1.00 = 25.0 psi Mortar: Type S · Wall: Hollow/Ungrouted D/C = 23.9 25.0 = 0.96 Buckling Safeguard — P vs ¼Pe Pe = 148957.9 lb/ft (e = 0 assumed, concentric axial — Eq. 8-14) TMS 402-22 §8.2.4.1 Eq. (8-10) P = 1000.0 plf ¼Pe = 37239.5 plf 0.03 PASS ▶ Calc TMS 402-22 §8.2.4.1 — Buckling Safeguard, Eq. (8-10) & Eq. (8-14) Eq. (8-10) limit: P ≤ ¼P e Em = 900 × fʼm (TMS 402-22 §4.2.2) = 900 × 1500.0 = 1350000.0 psi Eq. (8-14): P e = π²E m I n /h² × [1 − 0.577(e/r)]³ Assumption: concentric axial load — this calculator has no eccentricity input and models axial load as concentric, so e = 0 and [1 − 0.577(e/r)]³ = 1 I n = 130.4 in⁴/ft (from Section Properties table) = π² × 1350000.0 × 130.4 / 108.0² × 1 = 148957.9 lb/ft ¼Pe = 148957.9 / 4 = 37239.5 lb/ft P = 1000.0 plf (applied axial load) D/C = 1000.0 37239.5 = 0.027 Out-of-Plane Shear fv vs Fv V = 112.5 lb/ft; Fv governed by Table 8.2.6.2 (P/Aₙ term) TMS 402-22 §8.2.6 fv = 7.0 psi Fv = 55.8 psi 0.13 PASS ▶ Calc TMS 402-22 §8.2.6 — Out-of-Plane Shear V = w·h/2 (simply-supported reaction statics) = 25.0 × 9.0 / 2 = 112.5 lb/ft fv = (MDG Eq. 11.3-1; TMS 402-22 §8.2.6.1 Eq. 8-15 commentary for rectangular sections) 3 2 × V/A n = 1.5 × 112.5 / 24.0 = 7.0 psi Fv = min of §8.2.6.2(a)–(c): (a) 1.5 × √ 1500.0 (1.5√ fʼm ) = 58.1 psi (b) 120 psi (cap) (c) Table 8.2.6.2, running bond, other than fully grouted: 37 + 0.45 × P/A n = 37 + 0.45 × 41.7 = 55.8 psi Governs: F v = min(58.1, 120, 55.8) = 55.8 psi D/C = 7.0 55.8 = 0.126 Disclosure: fv = 1.5V/An is the rectangular-section stress (MDG Eq. 11.3-1) using the net area A n already computed above. TMS 402-22 §8.2.6.3 additionally requires that, for hollow units with an ASTM C140 normalized web area < 25 in.²/ft², the shear stress calculated in the webs (not this face-shell-bedded A n ) shall not exceed §8.2.6.2(a). This calculator does not collect unit web geometry (web count/thickness/height) and cannot perform that web-specific check — per MDG Example 11.3-2 commentary it rarely governs, but verify separately for thin-web/high-efficiency CMU. Engineering Notes & Assumptions: All calculations per TMS 402-22 (Building Code Requirements and Specification for Masonry Structures) and MDG-2022. Section properties use r = t/√12 (simplified, slightly conservative) per standard ASD masonry practice. Hollow (ungrouted) area based on face-shell bedding only: Aₙ = 2 × t₟s × 12 in²/ft. Net I and S based on hollow box section. P is the total ASD vertical load (dead + applicable live) at the critical section. Run a separate check with minimum P (0.6D only) to evaluate the tension case under wind. No wind 1/3 stress increase applied. Check TMS 402-22 §8.1.4.1 to confirm. Moment assumes wall simply supported at top and bottom for M = wh²/8. Verify boundary conditions for your project. Shear demand V = wh/2 (simply-supported reaction statics) is used for the out-of-plane shear check regardless of the selected support condition. This is exact for simply-supported and fixed-fixed walls; for fixed-pinned walls the true fixed-end reaction can be as high as 5wh/8 — verify shear independently for that case. Self-weight of wall is NOT automatically added to P. Include it as part of the input P if required at the critical section. TMS 402-22 Table 8.2.5.2 Fₜ values are for in-plane running bond, normal to bed joints (bending about horizontal axis as occurs here). Out-of-plane shear (TMS 402-22 §8.2.6) is checked as fv = 1.5·V/An against the smaller of 1.5√fʼm, 120 psi, and the Table 8.2.6.2 running-bond allowable. This calculator assumes running bond and does NOT perform the ASTM C140 normalized web-area check of §8.2.6.3 — for hollow units, verify the web shear stress separately if the normalized web area is less than 25 in.²/ft² (rarely governs per MDG Example 11.3-2, but must be checked). Buckling safeguard per TMS 402-22 §8.2.4.1 Eq. (8-10)/(8-14) assumes concentric axial load (e = 0, i.e., [1 − 0.577e/r]³ = 1) since this calculator has no eccentricity input. If the applied load is eccentric, recompute Pₑ using the actual eccentricity. This calculator checks out-of-plane bending, out-of-plane shear, and the axial buckling safeguard. In-plane shear and in-plane loading are outside its scope and must be checked separately per TMS 402-22 Chapter 8. For walls with large openings, pilasters, or non-uniform loading, additional analysis is required beyond this uniform-wall check. ⇄ Results Hub ⇄ Results Hub×
